name: Chicken Florentine
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Fresh spinach, washed and chopped coarsely
1/2 package Frozen Spinach, defrosted and squeezed of all water
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim all gristle and fat from the chicken breasts. Cut breasts in half and butterfly each half by slicing lengthwise, leaving one side uncut.
2. Place the chicken halves on waxed paper and gently flatten with a meat mallet until pieces are quite thin. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Steam fresh spinach in 3/4 cup salted water for 3 minutes and drain well. If using frozen spinach, simply drain well when completely defrosted.
4. Place equal amounts of spinach in the center of each half of chicken, cover with a slice of ham, and press down edges of chicken together. Seal edges with a meat mallet.
5. Beat eggs in a mixing bowl, add cream, parsley, and Parmesan cheese, and blend well.
6. Dust chicken lightly with flour and dip into egg batter one piece at a time, making sure to coat heavily.
7. Heat oil in a skillet until very hot, then saute each piece separately until crisp and golden in color. Trim excess egg batter and transfer chicken to a warm platter.
8. Melt butter in the frying pan large enough to accommodate all chicken. Add wine and cook until it evaporates—about 5-6 minutes.
9. Add brown sauce, cook for 5 minutes. Place chicken in the pan, cook for 5 minutes. Add lemon juice and cook an additional 3 minutes.
10. Turn the chicken once and cook another 5 minutes.
11. Place chicken breasts on a serving platter and pour hot sauce over to serve.
